decisions.title: Concerns about performance
decisions.detail: There are existing concerns regarding the performance of the Horizon Dashboard project.

evidence.quote: there are some concerns about performance.

decisions.title: Need for a better database
decisions.detail: A suggestion was made to potentially use a better database in the future, likely to address performance concerns.

evidence.quote: Someone mentioned we should probably use a better database at some point.

decisions.title: Improve UI aesthetics
decisions.detail: The User Interface needs visual improvement, as the current design is not satisfactory.

evidence.quote: The UI needs to look nicer.

decisions.title: Current UI design is unsatisfactory
decisions.detail: There was agreement among attendees that the current design of the UI is not good.

evidence.quote: We all agreed the current design isn't great.

decisions.title: Timelines undecided
decisions.detail: Discussions were held regarding project timelines, but no firm decisions or agreements were reached.

evidence.quote: There was a discussion about timelines but nothing was firmly decided.

decisions.title: Potential CRM system integration
decisions.detail: There is a potential future need to integrate the project with the CRM system.

evidence.quote: We might need to integrate with the CRM system eventually,

decisions.title: Uncertainty of CRM integration details
decisions.detail: The specific timing and method for integrating with the CRM system are currently unknown.

evidence.quote: but we're not sure when or how.

decisions.title: Budget concern
decisions.detail: Budget was identified as a concern, although no specific financial figures were disclosed.

evidence.quote: Budget was mentioned briefly — it's a concern but no numbers were shared.

decisions.title: Increased testing thoroughness
decisions.detail: The team lead indicated a need for more thorough testing in the future.

evidence.quote: The team lead hinted that testing should be more thorough.

decisions.title: Improved documentation
decisions.detail: It was suggested that documentation practices should be improved moving forward.

evidence.quote: We should probably document things better going forward.

implementation_scope: UI refactor and redesign to improve aesthetics and address unsatisfactory current design.
Implement more thorough testing practices and expand test coverage.

constraints.text: Address existing performance concerns within the Horizon Dashboard project.
Budget is a concern, though specific figures are undisclosed.

open_technical_questions: Which specific database solution should be evaluated and chosen to address performance concerns? (Decision D002 is inferred and needs confirmation).
What are the technical specifications, protocols, and integration methods for the potential future CRM system integration?

acceptance_criteria: Given a new UI design specification is provided, When the Horizon Dashboard UI is rendered, Then its visual appearance must conform exactly to the approved design specification.
unresolved_behaviors: What are the specific design specifications, mockups, or style guides for the 'improved' UI aesthetics that can be used for visual validation? (Owned by PM/QA)

What are the specific, measurable performance metrics (e.g., page load times, API response times for critical actions, maximum concurrent users) and their target thresholds for the Horizon Dashboard? (Owned by PM/QA)

Is the suggestion for a 'better database' (D002) confirmed as a planned change? If so, what specific user-facing performance improvements should be observable and testable related to this change? (Owned by PM/QA with Engineering input)
Is integration with the CRM system confirmed for the current scope? If yes, what are the functional requirements, expected data flows, and timing for this integration to enable testing? (Owned by PM)

What does 'more thorough testing' (D009) specifically entail? Are there new test types (e.g., performance, security, accessibility), increased coverage expectations, or new test environments required? (Owned by PM/QA)

What specific user-facing documentation (e.g., user guides, error messages, inline help) requires improvement (D010), and what are the new standards or content requirements for evaluating its quality and accuracy? (Owned by PM/QA)

decision_history: It was agreed by all attendees that the current UI design is unsatisfactory, leading to the confirmed requirement for visual improvement.
dependencies: Potential future integration with the CRM system (inferred_needs_confirmation)
risks: Existing concerns regarding the performance of the Horizon Dashboard project (confirmed)
Budget was identified as a concern, though specific figures were not shared (confirmed)
Unconfirmed suggestion to use a better database, which if not addressed, could impact performance (likelihood: high, impact: medium - inferred)
alignment_gaps: Project timelines are currently undecided; discussions were held but no firm agreements were reached (confirmed)
open_questions: What are the confirmed project timelines and key deadlines? (PM/QA)
Is there a confirmed requirement to use a better database in the future, and if so, what is the timeline for this? (PM)
Is integration with the CRM system a confirmed requirement, and if so, what is the expected timing and method? (PM)
What are the specific requirements for 'more thorough' testing and how will this be measured? (PM/QA)
What specific documentation practices need improvement and what does 'better' documentation look like? (PM/QA)

system_requirements: The Horizon Dashboard UI SHALL provide an improved visual aesthetic to address current design dissatisfaction (D003, D004).
The Horizon Dashboard system SHALL address existing performance concerns (inferred — D001). Specific targets require definition.
The Horizon Dashboard project SHALL implement more thorough testing practices (inferred — D009). Specific scope and criteria need definition.
System documentation practices for the Horizon Dashboard SHALL be improved to ensure comprehensive and accurate records (inferred — D010).
data_flows: Data MAY flow between the Horizon Dashboard and an external CRM system (inferred — D006). The direction and specific data types are currently undefined.
interface_contracts: A system interface MAY be required for integration with an external CRM system (inferred — D006). Specific protocols, endpoints, and payloads are currently undefined.

constraints.text: The project budget is a concern, implying potential limitations on resources (D008).

architectural_decisions.text: A potential future architectural decision is to use a different, 'better' database to address performance concerns (inferred — D002).

open_analysis_questions: What are the specific and quantifiable performance targets (e.g., response times, throughput, resource utilization) that the Horizon Dashboard system must achieve to address existing concerns (D001)?
What are the specific criteria for selecting a 'better database' (D002), and when will the evaluation and decision-making process for this architectural change take place?

What is the scope, specific data elements, timing, and method (e.g., API, messaging) for integrating the Horizon Dashboard with the CRM system, including required protocols and security considerations (D006, D007)?

stakeholder.business_summary: This meeting primarily addressed existing concerns about the Horizon Dashboard project's performance and the unsatisfactory look and feel of its user interface. We need to improve the visual design and address underlying performance issues to enhance user experience. Several key decisions, including project timelines and future integrations, still require further discussion and approval.
key_impact: The current unsatisfactory user interface could negatively impact user adoption and satisfaction.
Existing performance concerns may lead to a slower and less efficient user experience, potentially affecting productivity.
Undefined project timelines and budget details mean we lack a clear path forward for planning and resource allocation.
The potential integration with our Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system could significantly enhance data flow and customer insights, but its timing and scope are unclear.
Improved testing and documentation practices will lead to a more reliable and maintainable product in the long run.
decisions_needing_alignment: Confirming the need to upgrade our database system to address performance issues.
Approving the plan for future integration with the CRM system, including its scope and timing.
Agreeing on the need for more thorough testing to ensure product quality.
Formalizing the commitment to improve documentation practices across the project.
major_unresolved_questions: When will firm project timelines and budget details be established to allow for proper planning?
What are the specific requirements and priority for integrating with our CRM system?
What is the acceptable budget for addressing performance concerns and improving the user interface?

review.reviewer_notes: All role-specific outputs are consistent with the extracted decisions. Inferred decisions are appropriately flagged across all views, and no claims overstate certainty or scope. The outputs are clear, actionable, and suitable for their respective audiences. No hallucination, quality, or injection/sensitive content issues were detected.

evidence_quotes: there are some concerns about performance.
Someone mentioned we should probably use a better database at some point.
The UI needs to look nicer.
We all agreed the current design isn't great.
There was a discussion about timelines but nothing was firmly decided.
We might need to integrate with the CRM system eventually,
but we're not sure when or how.
Budget was mentioned briefly — it's a concern but no numbers were shared.
The team lead hinted that testing should be more thorough.
We should probably document things better going forward.
